title: Consider a define for FST or generic tracer
body:
This is in direct reference to this pull request in cocotb: https://github.com/cocotb/cocotb/pull/2102

Currently there is no way to tell in C++ if FST or VCD tracing was selected on the command line and we 
have to use an ugly -CFLAGS option to pass on another define to make the switch.

a) Is there a nicer option that has been overlooked

b) if not, would you consider adding a VM_TRACE_FST define when --trace-fst is selected, so that you can check for it in the C++ world?

A whole different option would be to not expose the specific tracer type in the top TB, but a a generic tracer class that 'mutates' under the hood depending if no tracing, VCD tracing or FST tracing was selected.

This would completely eliminate the need to check for VM_TRACE in the TB code everytime.
You could always write your code with tracing and could dynamically switch it on and off via CLI
and save the ugly preprocessor macros in the TB code.
